story: Twilight faded as the hunter climbed to the mountaintop bastion, called out, and was admitted by unseen magic. Led through halls that were eerie yet oddly homely, he reached a warm grand solar where a powdered fledgling vampire reclined by the hearth. The hunter set his saw-blade against the wall—talk first, always—and they fenced with words as the vampire sipped something red. The welcome was theatrical, almost kind. Before the hunter arrived, the vampire stitched a binding glamour into the rug by the hearth; one step onto the woven sigils would hold a guest fast until he spoke a releasing word. “Let’s speak plainly,” the hunter said. He had come to make a deal.
implausible_knowledge: Before the hunter arrived, the vampire stitched a binding glamour into the rug by the hearth; one step onto the woven sigils would hold a guest fast until he spoke a releasing word.
question: As he begins to make his offer in the solar, how will the hunter position himself and his weapon?
answer_with_knowledge: Keep his blade in hand and stay by the doorway, insisting they talk away from the hearth rug.
answer_without_knowledge: Leave his blade against the wall and step closer to the hearth to speak plainly.
